text.title: Puppeteer Page Interaction (Selectors, Clicks, Forms) Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the puppeteer-page-interaction methodology. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Interaction helpers MUST accept only attribute-based selectors (data-testid, id, name, role, aria-label). Positional selectors (nth-child) and visual class names are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Attribute-based selectors survive CSS refactors; positional/visual selectors break on the next layout change.

rule.statement: Scripts MUST NOT call page.waitForTimeout. Use waitForSelector / waitForFunction / waitForResponse targeting the actual condition.
rule.rationale: waitForTimeout was removed in Puppeteer 22 and was always flaky; targeted waits adapt to actual readiness.

rule.statement: Before click/type/select, the helper MUST waitForSelector({ visible: true }) on the target and check that it is not disabled. Direct .click() on a not-yet-ready element is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Race conditions on SPAs cause silent click misses; explicit waits make readiness observable.

rule.statement: Scraping scripts MUST set page.setRequestInterception(true) and block analytics/ads/tracking domains. Letting full analytics traffic flow during scrapes is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Analytics adds noise to waits, slows page, and triggers anti-bot scoring; blocking known domains makes runs faster and more stable.

rule.statement: Form fills MUST use the dedicated method for the element kind: type for text/email, select for <select>, click for checkbox/radio, and waitForResponse to confirm submission. JavaScript-driven value assignment is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Setting .value directly skips framework change-events (React, Vue) and leaves state inconsistent.

rule.statement: page.evaluate MUST be used only to read structured data, never to perform actions (click, fill) that have a Puppeteer API equivalent.
rule.rationale: page.evaluate clicks bypass Puppeteer's request/wait coordination; resulting state is racy.
